tluaNumberKeyIndexCoexistence.tlua(18,12): error TS7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expression of type '5' can't be used to index type 'U'.
  Property '5' does not exist on type 'U'.


==== tluaNumberKeyIndexCoexistence.tlua (1 errors) ====
    // Number keys and string keys are disjoint, including for index signatures:
    // a string index signature serves only string-key access, not number-key
    // access (t[5] != t["5"]).
    interface U {
      [k: string]: string | number;
      lit: number;
      1: string;
    }
    declare u: U;
    
    // The number key resolves to the number-keyed member.
    local u1: string = u[1];
    
    // A string-key access falls to the string index signature.
    local us: string | number = u["5"];
    
    // A number-key access is NOT covered by the string index signature: error.
    local un = u[5];
               ~~~~
!!! error TS7053: Element implicitly has an 'any' type because expression of type '5' can't be used to index type 'U'.
!!! error TS7053:   Property '5' does not exist on type 'U'.
    
    local es = {u1, us, un};
